title: SPARQL for NaN values fails
description:
i think there's a bug in the query parser when trying to query for NaN values:
select ?s where {
  ?s ?p "NaN"^^<http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#float> .
}
limit 10

try on dbpedia
Virtuoso 22005 Error SR334: Invalid floating point value converting 'NaN'

SPARQL query:
define sql:big-data-const 0
#output-format:text/html
define sql:signal-void-variables 1 define input:default-graph-uri <http://dbpedia.org> select ?s where {
  ?s ?p "NaN"^^<http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#float> .
}
limit 10
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE test_floats (id INT PRIMARY KEY, value FLOAT, description VARCHAR);
INSERT INTO test_floats (id, value, description) VALUES (1, CAST('NaN' AS FLOAT), 'Test NaN value');
INSERT INTO test_floats (id, value, description) VALUES (2, NULL, 'Test NULL value');
SELECT * FROM test_floats WHERE value IS NULL;
SELECT * FROM test_floats WHERE value IS NAN;
key_bug_features: Query parser fails with NaN values in SPARQL
